title: Ghana Application Form for Grant of a Visa and Permit for Return to Ghana
description: Apply through the Ghana Immigration Service (GIS) for a Re-Entry Visa/Permit — the 'APPLICATION FORM FOR GRANT OF A VISA/PERMIT FOR RETURN TO GHANA' — used by a person already resident in or connected to Ghana (a foreign national holding a Ghanaian residence/work permit, or a Ghanaian citizen/permit holder abroad) who wishes to travel out of Ghana temporarily and be guaranteed re-entry on return, opening Ghana's Visa vertical (1 of 6). This is scoped strictly to GIS's Re-Entry Visa/Permit pathway (the 'Re-Entry Visa' service listed at gis.gov.gh/visas/) and explicitly does NOT cover Ghana's first-time tourist e-Visa, which is issued exclusively through a login-gated online portal (evisa.immigration.gov.gh) with no downloadable static specimen — see VERIFICATION.md for the full scoping disclosure. The source is a 2-page scanned specimen distributed as a flattened raster image PDF (0 extractable text layer, 0 AcroForm widgets — every printed character is part of a full-page JPEG scan), fetched directly from GIS's own public forms library (gis.gov.gh/gis-forms/) and transcribed visually field-by-field after extracting the embedded JPEGs directly from the PDF's DCTDecode image XObjects (pdfjs-dist's canvas rendering path itself failed on this specimen's inline-image draw call in this environment; the raw JPEG streams were extracted and read directly instead — see VERIFICATION.md). Page 1 carries all 14 numbered applicant-facing items (name, previous name, nationality, date/place of birth, passport particulars, Ghana address, overseas address, education/occupation, residency duration and arrival dates, destination abroad, departure date, purpose and length of stay, reasons for return, marital status, and spouse particulars), a declaration statement, a signature/thumbprint-and-date line, and an ATTACHMENTS section (two passport photographs). Page 2 is headed 'FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY' and carries only GIS staff/cashier fields (fee paid, receipt number, cashier signature, and an office stamp) — deliberately excluded from fields[] as staff-populated, not applicant-facing; see VERIFICATION.md. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of Ghana or the Ghana Immigration Service.

source.documentRef: Ghana Immigration Service, "Application Form for Grant of a Visa/Permit for Return to Ghana" (Re-Entry Visa/Permit specimen), 2-page scanned/rasterized PDF, no form number printed on the specimen itself.

verification.notes: GovSchema Standard Research cycle GOV-2698, opening Ghana's Visa vertical (1 of 6) alongside sibling issues scouting/authoring Ghana's Passport (GOV-2696) and Business Formation (GOV-2697) verticals in parallel; Ghana itself opened as this registry's 41st jurisdiction via National ID (GOV-2510, gh/nia/ghana-card-application-form-1a). Independently re-fetched the source directly this cycle: HTTP 200, Content-Type application/pdf, 1,500,822 bytes, Last-Modified Fri 13 Jun 2025 11:12:54 GMT, sha256 14bdb332f24b1fd172db3b851fa369b2235d9ba9710f58cbbc545b5f9fdc470b — re-derived independently rather than trusting the issue's inherited URL string. pdfjs-dist (legacy build, v6) getAnnotations({intent:'display'}) confirms 0 Widget annotations across both pages, and getOperatorList() shows each page's entire content is one paintImageXObject call over a single full-page DCTDecode (plain JPEG) image (1652x2337 on page 1, 1240x1753 on page 2) with no other drawing operators except a clipping path — a genuinely flat, scanned/hand-fill specimen, not a mis-flattened AcroForm. Rendering the page through pdfjs-dist's canvas pipeline (node-canvas) itself failed in this environment ('Image or Canvas expected' inside pdfjs's internal paintInlineImageXObject helper, which its paintImageXObject path delegates to for JPEG XObjects) even after installing the canvas/Image/ImageData/Path2D polyfills this registry's other scanned-PDF schemas have used; rather than giving up on visual transcription, the two JPEG streams (PDF objects 14 and 22, /Filter /DCTDecode, lengths 1,360,539 and 133,520 bytes respectively) were located by direct byte-offset extraction from the raw PDF (locating each object's `stream`/`endstream` markers and slicing exactly /Length bytes) and written out as standalone .jpg files, confirmed valid via their JPEG magic bytes (FF D8 FF E0) — then read and transcribed directly. Every one of the 14 numbered items on page 1 was read directly from the extracted page-1 JPEG; page 2's 'FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY' block (Fee paid, Receipt No., Signature of Cashier, STAMP) was read from the extracted page-2 JPEG and is disclosed as excluded, staff-populated content, not modeled in fields[]. Two disclosed field-decomposition judgment calls: (1) item 5's own header line ('ADDRESS IN GHANA (IN FULL)....') carries its own dotted answer space distinct from and preceding its own lettered (a) Postal/(b) Residential sub-items (unlike items 3/4/8, whose lettered sub-parts begin immediately with no separate header blank) — modeled as its own required addressInGhana field rather than treated as a non-fillable section title; (2) item 11 ('OBJECT OF JOURNEY AND PROPOSED LENGTH OF STAY ABROAD') prints no (a)/(b) lettering, unlike items 3/4/8, so it is modeled as a single combined field rather than split into separate purpose/duration fields. maritalStatus (item 13) prints no enumerated options anywhere on the specimen (unlike, e.g., ng/nis's sibling form, whose enum was taken from an explicit second-edition option list) — modeled as free-text string rather than inventing an enum boundary the source does not provide; spouse name/nationality (item 14) are therefore left optional and NOT machine-gated on maritalStatus for the same reason. Scoping disclosure: this schema covers ONLY the Re-Entry Visa/Permit pathway (matching the 'Re-Entry Visa'/'Re-entry permit' service independently confirmed live at gis.gov.gh/visas/, described there as for 'visitors who desire to leave [Ghana] temporarily and return'); it does NOT cover Ghana's first-time tourist e-Visa, independently re-confirmed this cycle to be a live but exclusively online, account/login-gated wizard at evisa.immigration.gov.gh with no downloadable unauthenticated specimen of any kind. Conformance run: 2 valid mock scenarios (0 errors each) and 3 mutation-control fixtures (a missing-required-field case, a pattern violation, and an enum violation, each raising exactly 1 error) using a disposable validate_instance.mjs script (not committed) implementing this schema's own required/requiredWhen grammar plus ajv for the static JSON Schema subset — see VERIFICATION.md for the full write-up. No duplicate-concurrent-run PR for this same GIS form was found open in the repo as of this cycle.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Mr./Mrs./Miss
fields.description: The source prints exactly three honorific options ('MR./MRS./MISS') directly before item 1's name blank, a closed, bounded vocabulary the applicant selects from; modeled as its own enum field (cf. za/dha/smart-id-card-application's own 'title' enum), distinct from ng/nis's sibling Visa schema, which kept an equivalent prefix embedded in a free-text label since that form's own printed prefix had no separately identifiable closed set.

fields.name: previousName
fields.label: Previous name (if any)
fields.description: Not required: an applicant with no previous name has nothing to declare, and the source provides no separate 'none' checkbox.

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Date of birth
fields.description: Item 3(b) combines date and place of birth on one printed blank ('DATE & PLACE OF BIRTH'); split here into two machine fields for reusability.

fields.name: passportIssueDate
fields.label: Date of issue
fields.description: Item 4(b) combines date and place of issue on one printed blank ('DATE & PLACE OF ISSUE'); split here into two machine fields.

fields.name: addressInGhana
fields.label: Address in Ghana (in full)
fields.description: Item 5's own header line carries its own dotted answer space, printed before and distinct from its own lettered (a) Postal/(b) Residential sub-items below — unlike items 3/4/8, whose lettered sub-parts begin immediately with no separate header blank. Modeled as its own field (a general/primary Ghana address) rather than treated as a non-fillable section title; disclosed judgment call, see VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: postalAddressGhana
fields.label: Postal address in Ghana (Office/Business)
fields.description: Not required: an applicant without an office/business postal address (e.g. unemployed, retired, student) has nothing to declare here.

fields.name: residentialAddressGhanaTelephone
fields.label: Telephone No. (residential address)
fields.description: The source prints no format instruction beyond the blank; a generic, GovSchema-added phone-character pattern (digits, spaces, and the common +()- separators) is applied as a reasonable technical validation aid, not a claim about an official Ghanaian telephone-numbering standard.

fields.name: overseasAddressTelephone
fields.label: Telephone No. (overseas address)
fields.description: Not required: a telephone number is not always available at the applicant's overseas address.

fields.name: yearsResidentInGhana
fields.label: How long resident in Ghana
fields.description: Modeled as free text since the source prints no unit instruction (years/months) beyond the blank.

fields.name: destinationAbroad
fields.label: Destination abroad (give full address)
fields.description: Printed with a two-line answer space; modeled as a single free-text field.

fields.name: objectOfJourneyAndLengthOfStay
fields.label: Object of journey and proposed length of stay abroad
fields.description: Item 11 prints no (a)/(b) lettering (unlike items 3/4/8), so this is modeled as a single combined free-text field rather than split into separate purpose/duration fields.
fields.sourceRef: Item 11: "OBJECT OF JOURNEY AND PROPOSED LENGTH OF STAY ABROAD.................."

fields.name: reasonsForReturnToGhana
fields.label: Reasons for wishing to return to Ghana
fields.description: Printed with a three-line answer space and the instruction 'TO BE SUPPORTED BY DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE' beneath it — see the sibling reasonsForReturnSupportingEvidence document.
fields.sourceRef: Item 12: "REASONS FOR WISHING TO RETURN TO GHANA.................. (TO BE SUPPORTED BY DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE)"

fields.name: maritalStatus
fields.label: Marital status
fields.description: The source prints no enumerated option list for this item (unlike, e.g., ng/nis's sibling Visa schema, whose enum was taken directly from a second edition's explicit option list); modeled as free text rather than inventing an enum boundary not present on this specimen.

fields.name: spouseName
fields.label: Name of spouse
fields.description: Applicable only if married; not machine-gated on maritalStatus since that sibling field is free text with no reliable machine match against a 'married' value.

fields.name: signatureDate
fields.label: Signature date
fields.sourceRef: Page 1, below item 14: "DATE" beside the (unwidgeted) signature/thumbprint line

documents.label: Two (2) current passport-size photographs
documents.sourceRef: Page 1, ATTACHMENTS section, item 1: "TWO (2) CURRENT PASSPORT SIZE PHOTOGRAPHS."; a blank ruled box in the form's top-right margin is provided for affixing one photograph.

documents.label: Documentary evidence supporting reasons for return to Ghana
documents.sourceRef: Page 1, beneath item 12: "(TO BE SUPPORTED BY DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE)"

documents.label: Declaration of truthfulness (signature/thumbprint)
documents.statement: I SOLEMNLY DECLARE THAT THE ABOVE PARTICULARS GIVEN BY ME ARE TRUE IN SUBSTANCE AND FACT
documents.sourceRef: Page 1, beneath item 14, beside the (unwidgeted) 'SIGNATURE/THUMBPRINT OF APPLICANT' line and 'DATE' blank.
